text: Last class I kept the meeting on track and moved on.
Today I want to recap the decisions and assign the tasks.

Karen, you will send the report by Friday.
Daniel will confirm the budget by Monday.
I will follow up with both of you by Tuesday.
Who will be the owner of this action item?
Karen, let me recap your action item. You are the owner of the final report.
Yes. I will send the report by Friday. Should I add the budget numbers?
Good question. Daniel will confirm the budget by Monday, so add them after that.
Perfect. So the main takeaway is: report on Friday, numbers after Monday.
Exactly. I will follow up with both of you on Tuesday. Those are our next steps.
Great. Thank you, Percival. I will start on the report today.
Let me recap the decisions before we finish.
I will follow up with you on Tuesday.
Those are our next steps. Thank you all.

Alright, everyone, we are at the end of the meeting, so let me recap. First, we made two decisions today. We will use the new supplier, and we will move the launch to October. Now, the action items. Karen, you will send the final report by Friday. You are the owner of that task. Daniel, you will confirm the budget with the board by Monday. I will follow up with both of you on Tuesday. Those are our next steps. Thank you all, and I will see you next week.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I am calling about my action item from the meeting. You asked me to send the final report by Friday, and I am the owner of that task. I have good news. I will finish it by Thursday instead, one day early. Could you confirm one thing for me, please? Is Daniel still going to confirm the budget by Monday? I want to add his number to the report. Thank you, and I will follow up by email today. Bye.

First, Percival closes the meeting and starts the recap. Then, he summarizes the two decisions of the day. After that, he assigns the action items to each owner. Later, he sets a deadline for every task with by Friday and by Monday. Finally, he says he will follow up and confirms the next steps.
